project.name: tamu-shpe-admin-web
project.description: Internal admin portal for the Texas A&M SHPE chapter. Officers use this Next.js app to manage the same Firebase backend as the chapter mobile app for events, membership verification, points, committees, and operational tools. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Shirt pickup tracker page with searchable member rows and toggle checkboxes for received status.

nodes.name: Badges.tsx
nodes.summary: Small status badge components for officer, verified, and neutral membership labels.

nodes.name: ConfirmDialog.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable confirmation dialog with cancel/confirm actions for destructive or irreversible operations.

nodes.name: DataTable.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic sortable data table with column definitions, sticky headers, and optional empty/loading states.

nodes.name: ShirtTrackerPage
nodes.summary: Shirt tracker UI toggling pickup status per member via useShirts/useToggleShirt.

nodes.summary: Member multi-select dialog that awards Instagram points through a mutation hook.

nodes.summary: Lists Instagram point awards in a searchable DataTable with award dialog controls.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router catch-all that adapts the Hono app to Vercel/Node request handlers for GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE.
nodes.languageNotes: Forces nodejs runtime so Firebase Admin can run outside Edge.

nodes.name: test-conventions-route.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test script that seeds Firestore fixtures and exercises convention track/untrack routes against the emulator.

nodes.name: testTrackNewUids
nodes.summary: Verifies tracking new UIDs creates convention-tracking docs and returns them as tracked.

nodes.name: testRetrackAlreadyTracked
nodes.summary: Ensures re-tracking already tracked UIDs reports alreadyTracked without rewriting timestamps.

nodes.name: testMixedValidAndUnknown
nodes.summary: Checks mixed valid/unknown UID lists return partial success without creating docs for unknowns.

nodes.name: expectValidationError
nodes.summary: Helper that posts an invalid body and asserts a 400 validation error response.

nodes.name: testUntrack
nodes.summary: Verifies untrack removes convention-tracking docs for previously tracked UIDs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates fixture seed/cleanup and runs the full conventions route test suite.

nodes.name: test-db-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for chunked Firestore batch writes and cached event start-time lookups, including failure injection.

nodes.name: test251Edits
nodes.summary: Stress-tests chunkedAtomicBatch across more than one Firestore batch chunk.

nodes.name: testInjectedFailure
nodes.summary: Asserts ChunkedBatchError reports committed/failed batch indexes when a write fails mid-run.

nodes.name: testStartTimeCache
nodes.summary: Confirms makeEventStartTimeGetter caches Firestore reads for repeated event IDs.

nodes.name: testNotFound
nodes.summary: Asserts EventNotFoundError when requesting start time for a missing event doc.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Runs db-helper integration tests with scratch cleanup before and after.

nodes.name: test-events-route.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test script covering event create/update and single/bulk attendance approval against the events router.

nodes.name: testCreateEvent
nodes.summary: Creates a valid event via POST and asserts the returned document shape.

nodes.name: testInvalidEventType
nodes.summary: Asserts validation rejection for an unsupported event type payload.

nodes.name: testUpdateEvent
nodes.summary: Updates an existing event and verifies persisted field changes.

nodes.name: testSingleApprove
nodes.summary: Approves a single attendance log and checks log/user mirror updates.

nodes.name: testBulkApprove
nodes.summary: Bulk-approves multiple attendance UIDs in one request.

nodes.name: testBulkApproveMissingUid
nodes.summary: Ensures bulk approve fails when a requested UID has no attendance log.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Creates a disposable event, runs the events suite, then cleans up.

nodes.name: test-instagram-route.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for Instagram points awards, including first-event creation, re-awards, validation, and callable-shaped log compat.

nodes.name: cleanupFixtures
nodes.summary: Deletes seeded users, event logs, and Instagram events created during the suite.

nodes.name: testFirstAwardCreatesEvent
nodes.summary: Awards Instagram points and asserts a shared Instagram event plus dual logs are created.

nodes.name: testReAwardAccumulates
nodes.summary: Re-awards the same UID and verifies points/log accumulation on the existing event.

nodes.name: testMixedValidAndUnknown
nodes.summary: Awards a mix of valid and unknown UIDs and checks partial award behavior.

nodes.name: testCallableShapedLogCompat
nodes.summary: Ensures awards remain compatible with callable-shaped existing event log docs.

nodes.name: expectValidationError
nodes.summary: Posts invalid Instagram award bodies and asserts 400 validation errors.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Seeds fixtures, runs Instagram award tests, and always cleans up afterward.

nodes.name: test-membership-route.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for membership approve/deny flows, restoring seeded request state and verifying notification side effects.

nodes.name: testApprove
nodes.summary: Approves a seeded membership request and verifies user/request state transitions.

nodes.name: testDeny
nodes.summary: Denies a seeded membership request and verifies denied state and cleanup.

nodes.name: testUnknownUid
nodes.summary: Asserts approve/deny handling for a UID with no membership request.

nodes.name: restoreSeededState
nodes.summary: Restores the fixture membership request document between approve/deny tests.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Runs membership approve/deny/unknown tests with seeded state restore.

nodes.name: test-tools-route.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the tools router resume-visibility toggle, including invalid body and unknown-uid cases.

nodes.name: testToggleFlips
nodes.summary: Toggles resume visibility and asserts the boolean flips on the user document.

nodes.name: testNonBooleanBody
nodes.summary: Asserts validation error when the toggle body is not a boolean.

nodes.name: testUnknownUid
nodes.summary: Asserts not-found handling when toggling a missing user UID.

nodes.name: app.ts
nodes.summary: Hono application factory that mounts auth middleware and registers points, tools, membership, events, conventions, and Instagram routers under /api.

nodes.name: firebaseAdmin.ts
nodes.summary: Initializes the Firebase Admin app for emulator or production and exports shared adminAuth and adminDb clients.

nodes.name: buildApp
nodes.summary: Constructs Firebase Admin app using emulator settings or production service credentials.

nodes.name: cloudFunctions.ts
nodes.summary: Stub layer for remote Cloud Functions (points recalculation and membership notifications), logging no-ops on the emulator.
nodes.languageNotes: Production paths throw until X3 wiring; emulator stubs return canned success.

nodes.name: updateAllUserPoints
nodes.summary: Stub that no-ops on emulator or throws until production Cloud Function wiring lands.

nodes.name: sendNotificationMemberSHPE
nodes.summary: Stub that logs membership decision notifications on emulator or throws in production until wired.

nodes.name: db-helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Firestore helpers for chunked atomic batch writes and memoized event start-time reads, with typed batch/not-found errors.

nodes.name: chunkedAtomicBatch
nodes.summary: Commits Firestore write ops in atomic chunks, throwing ChunkedBatchError on mid-run failure.

nodes.name: makeEventStartTimeGetter
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized async getter for event start times, throwing EventNotFoundError when missing.

nodes.name: ChunkedBatchError
nodes.summary: Error capturing which chunked batches committed before a write failure.

nodes.name: EventNotFoundError
nodes.summary: Error thrown when an event document cannot be found for start-time lookup.

nodes.summary: Hono middleware that verifies Firebase ID tokens (or accepts emulator bypass) and attaches uid to the request context.

nodes.name: conventions.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router for convention tracking and untracking of user UIDs via chunked Firestore batch writes.

nodes.name: events.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router for creating/updating SHPE events and approving attendance logs in single or bulk batches.

nodes.name: toFirestoreEventData
nodes.summary: Maps a validated event request body into Firestore event document fields.

nodes.summary: Hono router that awards Instagram points, creating a shared Instagram event on demand and writing dual-sided event logs.

nodes.name: getOrCreateInstagramEvent
nodes.summary: Finds or creates the shared Instagram points event and returns its id and sign-in points.

nodes.name: membership.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router for approving or denying MemberSHPE requests and notifying members via the Cloud Function stub.

nodes.name: points.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router for bulk points adjustments with event start-time lookup, chunked writes, and optional full points recalculation.

nodes.name: tools.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router exposing admin tools such as toggling resume visibility on a user document.

nodes.name: useEditPoints

nodes.name: useRecalculatePoints

nodes.name: VolunteerEvent
nodes.summary: Factory subclass for volunteer events scored by points per hour.

nodes.name: SocialEvent
nodes.summary: Factory subclass for social events with default sign-in points.

nodes.name: IntramuralEvent
nodes.summary: Factory subclass for intramural sports events.

nodes.name: CustomEvent
nodes.summary: Factory subclass for custom events with flexible points configuration.

nodes.name: getStatusMessage
nodes.summary: Maps an event/log status and UI mode to a user-facing status message string.

nodes.name: isMemberVerified
nodes.summary: Returns whether both national and chapter membership expirations are still valid.

nodes.name: getBadgeColor
nodes.summary: Picks a badge color based on officer and verified membership flags.

nodes.name: formatExpirationDate
nodes.summary: Formats a Firebase Timestamp expiration into a localized US date string.

nodes.name: deriveConventionCounts
nodes.summary: Aggregates verified event-log attendance into convention count buckets by event type.

nodes.name: isConventionEligible
nodes.summary: Checks whether derived counts meet the required threshold for each convention category.

nodes.name: fetchConventionData
nodes.summary: Loads tracked members, events, and logs from Firebase and computes eligibility rows.

nodes.name: useConventionTracking
nodes.summary: React Query hook exposing convention tracking rows for the admin UI.

nodes.name: useTrackMembers
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that POSTs member UIDs to start convention tracking via authedFetch.

nodes.name: useUntrackMember
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that removes a member from convention tracking via authedFetch.

nodes.name: fetchInstagramPointsData
nodes.summary: Queries Instagram event logs and user profiles from Firebase into sortable award rows.

nodes.name: useInstagramPoints
nodes.summary: React Query hook returning Instagram points award rows for the tools page.

nodes.name: useAwardInstagramPoints
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that awards Instagram points through an authenticated API call.

nodes.name: assertCounts
nodes.summary: Asserts derived convention count objects match expected values and logs pass/fail.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Committees admin page that loads committee data via useCommittees and renders a responsive card grid with loading, error, and empty states.

nodes.name: CommitteeGridSkeleton
nodes.summary: Renders a six-card skeleton grid shown while committee data is loading.

nodes.name: CommitteesPage
nodes.summary: Default page component that fetches committees and switches between skeleton, error, empty, and card-grid views.

nodes.name: CommitteeCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Presentational card for a single committee showing logo, description, member count, and lead avatars.

nodes.name: UserAvatar
nodes.summary: Small avatar that shows a user's photo or falls back to initials derived from their name.

nodes.name: CommitteeCard
nodes.summary: Exported card UI that resolves committee logos and lists lead avatars with overflow handling.

nodes.name: skeleton.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable animated skeleton placeholder used as a loading shimmer across admin pages.

nodes.name: Skeleton
nodes.summary: Pulse-animated div that accepts className and HTML attributes for loading placeholders.

nodes.name: committees.ts
nodes.summary: Committee domain types, logo registry mapping Firebase names to React logo components, and name-formatting helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes TypeScript interfaces with a runtime logo component map and kebab-case name helpers.

nodes.name: getLogoComponent
nodes.summary: Looks up a committee logo React component by Firebase logo name from the committeeLogos registry.

nodes.name: reverseFormattedFirebaseName
nodes.summary: Converts a kebab-case Firebase committee name into title-cased display text.

nodes.name: user.ts
nodes.summary: Core user and membership TypeScript types plus shared constants for class years and majors used across the admin portal.

nodes.name: generateClassYears
nodes.summary: Builds a sliding window of graduation class-year strings relative to the current calendar year.

nodes.name: useCommittees.ts
nodes.summary: React Query hook that fetches committees from Firestore and resolves lead user references into PublicUserInfo.

nodes.name: resolvePublicUserInfo
nodes.summary: Resolves a lead that may be either embedded PublicUserInfo or a user document ID via a Firestore userInfo lookup.

nodes.name: fetchCommittees
nodes.summary: Loads the committees collection and hydrates each document's leads array into resolved public user profiles.

nodes.name: useCommittees
nodes.summary: Exported React Query wrapper exposing committees data, loading, error, and refetch for the committees page.

nodes.name: Providers.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side React wrapper that supplies a TanStack QueryClient to the app tree via QueryClientProvider.

nodes.name: Providers
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that resolves a QueryClient and wraps children in QueryClientProvider.

nodes.name: sonner.tsx
nodes.summary: Light-theme Sonner toast host with Lucide icons and shadcn-style toast classNames; skips next-themes.

nodes.name: Toaster
nodes.summary: Renders a Sonner toaster fixed to light theme with custom success, info, warning, error, and loading icons.

nodes.name: fonts.ts
nodes.summary: Defines Next.js Google font loaders (Oswald, Work Sans, Open Sans, Crimson Text) and a joined fontVariables class string for the root layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses next/font/google CSS variables rather than runtime font loading.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js root layout for the TAMU SHPE admin portal: metadata, font CSS variables, Providers, and global Toaster.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default root layout that applies fontVariables on html and wraps page children with Providers plus Toaster.

nodes.name: queryClient.ts
nodes.summary: TanStack Query client factory with admin-friendly defaults and SSR-safe browser singleton access via getQueryClient.
nodes.languageNotes: Creates a fresh QueryClient per server request; reuses one browser instance to preserve cache under React Strict Mode.

nodes.name: makeQueryClient
nodes.summary: Internal factory that builds a QueryClient with 60s staleTime and a single retry on query failure.

nodes.name: getQueryClient
nodes.summary: Returns a new QueryClient on the server and a lazy browser singleton so remounts do not reset the cache.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Local Docker Compose stack that runs the Firebase Emulator Suite and the Next.js admin web app together, with shared volumes and emulator-only environment variables.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-service compose file: emulators (Firebase) and web (bun install, seed, next dev), with named volumes for node_modules and .next.

nodes.name: emulators
nodes.summary: Firebase Emulator Suite service exposing UI, Firestore, Auth, and Storage ports, importing seed data and exporting on exit.

nodes.name: web
nodes.summary: Dev web service that installs dependencies, waits for Firestore, seeds fixtures, then runs bun dev against the emulator hosts.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Dev container image based on Bun with JRE, Node, and firebase-tools for running the Firebase Emulator Suite and the admin portal locally.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-stage image; app code is bind-mounted at runtime rather than COPY'd into the image.

nodes.name: bun
nodes.summary: Primary image stage from oven/bun:1.3 that installs Java, Node 20, and firebase-tools and exposes emulator and app ports.

nodes.name: .env.development
nodes.summary: Development environment variables for the Firebase web API key and local Auth, Firestore, and Storage emulator hosts used by client and Admin SDK init.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Standing AI-agent context for the TAMU SHPE admin portal: architecture hard rules, doc map, target layout aliases, stack, and optional implementer/scout orchestration.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview covering admin features by route, install/run steps, tech stack, app/ structure, related repos, and Vercel deployment.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui CLI configuration for RSC/TSX components, Tailwind paths (tailwind.config.ts, app/globals.css), and import aliases under app/components and lib.

nodes.name: firebase.json
nodes.summary: Firebase project config pointing at Firestore and Storage rules files and defining Auth, Firestore, Storage, and Emulator UI ports for local Docker-based development.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for tamu-shpe-admin-web defining Next.js scripts (dev/build/seed/typecheck) and dependencies including Firebase, Hono, TanStack Query, and shadcn/Radix UI.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript strict compiler config for the Next.js app with path aliases @/* → app/*, @/server/* → server/*, and @/lib/* → lib/*, excluding legacy and prototype trees.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Next.js TypeScript plugin and bundler moduleResolution with jsx preserve.

nodes.name: API.md
nodes.summary: HTTP API contract for the Hono write-only server layer, documenting auth middleware, route modules under /api/*, and clarifying which reads stay client-side via TanStack Query hooks.

nodes.name: BUILD_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Phased rebuild plan from scaffolding through go-live, with parallel tracks for membership, events, committees, tools, and points, plus testing and orchestration notes.

nodes.name: DATA_MODEL.md
nodes.summary: Source of truth for Firestore collection shapes and mirrored TypeScript types in app/types/, including dual-write invariants, membership rules, and the mobile type-sync checklist.

nodes.name: DESIGN_BRIEF.md
nodes.summary: UI/UX design brief for the officer admin portal covering Aggie brand tokens, layout shell, screen specs, reusable components, and polish priorities for a dense internal tool.

nodes.name: PURPOSE_AND_FUNCTIONALITY.md
nodes.summary: Product purpose and feature breakdown of the admin portal—auth model, Firestore usage, and detailed module behavior for dashboard, events, points, membership, committees, and tools.

nodes.name: REBUILD_CONCEPT.md
nodes.summary: Rebuild architecture guide defining the Hono server write boundary, client read hooks, directory layout, dual-write enforcement, emulator/Docker local setup, and Vercel deployment conventions.

nodes.name: tamu-design-system.html
nodes.summary: Standalone Texas A&M brand design-system reference page encoding maroon color tokens, Aggie typography (Oswald, Work Sans, Open Sans, Crimson Text), and marketing-site visual patterns used as inspiration for the portal UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained HTML/CSS demo with CSS custom properties for TAMU brand tokens; not wired into the Next.js app build.

nodes.name: implementer.md
nodes.summary: Claude agent prompt for spec-driven implementation of scoped units (Hono routes, TanStack Query hooks, shadcn pages) after architecture is settled. Enforces client-read / server-write boundaries and minimal on-scope changes.

nodes.name: scout.md
nodes.summary: Read-only Claude scout agent prompt for locating helpers, tracing original client behavior, and comparing source against rebuild docs with file:line citations. Never edits code.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: gitignore-style ignore patterns for the Understand-Anything knowledge-graph scanner, mostly commented suggestions for excluding build artifacts and sensitive paths.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Understand-Anything project config setting graph output language to English.

nodes.name: ua-scan-files.stderr
nodes.summary: Ephemeral stderr log from a project file scan (scan-project summary line); not application source.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global Tailwind and shadcn CSS variables mapping Aggie maroon brand tokens to light-theme design tokens for the admin portal.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses shadcn HSL CSS-variable convention with DESIGN_BRIEF brand hex mapped in comments.

nodes.name: firestore.rules
nodes.summary: Dev-only permissive Firestore security rules for the Firebase Emulator Suite; explicitly not for production deployment.

nodes.name: next.config.js
nodes.summary: Minimal Next.js config enabling React strict mode for the admin web app.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS pipeline wiring Tailwind CSS and Autoprefixer for stylesheet processing.

nodes.name: SHPE Admin Portal.dc.html
nodes.summary: Large Design-Companion HTML prototype of the SHPE Admin Portal UI (login and screens) with inline brand styles, loading generated support.js for runtime bindings.

nodes.name: support.js
nodes.summary: Bundled Design-Companion runtime (generated from dc-runtime) that powers reactive bindings and components in the HTML prototype; do-not-edit build artifact.
nodes.languageNotes: IIFE bundle with no tree-sitter-visible top-level exports; ~640 internal call edges.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Firebase Emulator seed script that creates officer/member Auth users, events with dual-located logs, membership requests, committees, resumes, and convention-tracking docs for local testing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses firebase-admin against emulator hosts only; aborts if emulator env vars are missing.

nodes.name: ensureAuthUser
nodes.summary: Creates an Auth user idempotently (ignores uid-already-exists) and optionally sets custom claims such as officer.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates full emulator seeding: officer and members, events/logs, memberSHPE requests, shirt sizes, committees, resumes, and convention tracking.

nodes.name: storage.rules
nodes.summary: Dev-only permissive Firebase Storage rules for the Emulator Suite; mirrors the firestore.rules non-production policy.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.ts
nodes.summary: Tailwind theme extension mapping shadcn CSS variables and Aggie brand colors (maroon, gold) for app and lib content paths.

layers.name: UI Presentation
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages, shared React components, shadcn primitives, and Design-Companion prototypes that render the SHPE officer admin portal.

layers.name: Client Data Access
layers.description: React Query hooks, Firebase web client setup, domain TypeScript types, and auth helpers that read Firestore and call the Hono write API from the browser.

layers.name: API Server
layers.description: Hono application, Firebase Admin, auth middleware, and domain write routes mounted through the Next.js catch-all API adapter.

layers.name: Data Rules
layers.description: Firestore and Storage security rules that define emulator and backend access constraints for chapter data shared with the mobile app.

layers.name: Tests & Seeding
layers.description: Emulator seed script and standalone integration/unit test scripts that exercise Hono routes, Firestore helpers, and points bucketing.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker image and Compose services that run the Firebase Emulator Suite alongside the Next.js admin web app for local development.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Package, TypeScript, Next.js, Tailwind, Firebase, shadcn, env, and analysis-tooling configs that build and run the admin portal.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Product, API, data-model, design, and rebuild docs plus Claude agent prompts and the TAMU design-system reference for the SHPE admin portal.

tour.title: Login Entry Point
tour.description: Officers land on app/page.tsx, the Next.js login screen for the TAMU SHPE admin portal. It offers Google sign-in plus an emulator path for local development, then redirects authorized officers into the main admin area. This is the human-facing front door before any dashboard or API traffic begins.
tour.languageLesson: In the Next.js App Router, app/page.tsx is the route for `/`. Client components that call Firebase Auth must be marked with 'use client' so browser APIs and hooks can run.

tour.title: Root Layout Shell
tour.description: Building on the login page, app/layout.tsx wraps every route with metadata, font CSS variables, and global UI chrome. Providers.tsx injects a TanStack QueryClient so later feature hooks share one cache. Together they establish the React tree every authenticated page hangs from.

tour.title: Auth Gate Layout
tour.description: Once signed in, app/(main)/layout.tsx protects officer routes by requiring Firebase auth and recognized claims. helpers/auth.ts centralizes Google/emulator sign-in, officer/admin checks, and sign-out. This gate is why dashboard, events, and tools pages never render for unauthenticated users.

tour.title: Hono API Mount
tour.description: Writes do not go straight from the browser to Firestore Admin APIs. server/app.ts builds a Hono app that mounts auth middleware and domain routers under /api. The catch-all app/api/[[...route]]/route.ts adapts that Hono app to Next.js request handlers on Vercel or Node, bridging the UI entry you just saw to the write-only server layer.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router catch-all segments like [[...route]] forward every /api/* path to one handler. Hono then dispatches by method and path inside that single adapter.

tour.title: Server Auth Layer
tour.description: Every Hono write route runs through middleware/auth.ts, which verifies Firebase ID tokens (or accepts an emulator bypass). firebaseAdmin.ts initializes the Admin SDK for emulator or production and exports shared Firestore access. This pair enforces that only authenticated officers can mutate chapter data shared with the mobile app.

tour.title: Domain Write Routes
tour.description: From the Hono factory (Step 4), domain routers own mutating operations: events creates/updates and attendance approval, points handles bulk score adjustments, and membership approves or denies MemberSHPE requests. Studying these three shows the project's write boundary—each feature's side effects live here, not in page components.

tour.title: Firestore Write Helpers
tour.description: Route handlers lean on db-helpers.ts for chunked atomic batch writes and memoized event start-time lookups. These utilities keep large points and convention updates under Firestore batch limits while remaining reusable across routers. They are the shared plumbing behind the domain writes from Step 6.

tour.title: Client Firebase Bridge
tour.description: Reads take a different path than writes. firebaseClient.ts initializes the browser Firebase app (Firestore, Auth, Storage, Analytics). authedFetch.ts attaches a Firebase ID token and POSTs JSON to the Hono /api layer. This split—client reads, authenticated server writes—is the core architectural rule of the admin portal.
tour.languageLesson: Using both the Firebase Web SDK (client) and Admin SDK (server) is intentional: the client observes Firestore in real time or via queries, while privileged mutations go through verified HTTP routes.

tour.title: Domain Types & Model
tour.description: TypeScript models in app/types/events.ts and user.ts mirror Firestore shapes officers manage daily. docs/DATA_MODEL.md is the source of truth for collections, dual-write invariants, and the mobile type-sync checklist. Learn these contracts before diving into hooks and screens so field names and rules make sense.

tour.title: React Query Hooks
tour.description: Feature hooks wrap the client bridge: useEvents lists and mutates events, usePoints buckets school-year scores and saves edits, useMembership loads requests and drives approve/deny. They call Firestore for reads and authedFetch for writes, giving every admin page a consistent data API on top of Steps 8–9.
tour.languageLesson: TanStack Query separates query keys and mutations so UI components stay declarative: hooks own caching, loading, and invalidation after Hono writes succeed.

tour.title: Core Admin Screens
tour.description: With hooks in place, the main officer workflows surface as App Router pages: events maps calendars and pending logs, points edits totals or monthly scores, and membership tabs pending requests versus official members. These screens compose shared UI primitives with the hooks from Step 10—no direct Admin SDK calls.

tour.title: Operational Tools Hub
tour.description: Beyond core chapter ops, the tools hub gathers resume zip downloads, shirt tracking, convention tracking, and Instagram points. useTools.ts and server/routes/tools.ts follow the same client-read / server-write pattern, showing how new operational features plug into the architecture without special-casing.

tour.title: Local Emulator Stack
tour.description: Local development runs Firebase Emulator Suite alongside Next.js via Docker. The Dockerfile installs Bun, JRE, Node, and firebase-tools; docker-compose.yml wires emulator and web services with shared volumes and emulator-only env vars. This stack lets officers and developers exercise auth and Firestore safely before touching production.
tour.languageLesson: Compose services isolate concerns: emulators expose Auth/Firestore/Storage ports while the web service waits on Firestore, seeds fixtures, then runs the Next.js dev server against those hosts.

tour.title: Architecture & API Docs
tour.description: Close the loop with the rebuild architecture guide and HTTP API contract. REBUILD_CONCEPT.md documents the Hono write boundary, client read hooks, emulator setup, and Vercel conventions you have now seen in code. API.md enumerates /api routes and clarifies which reads stay client-side—use both as the map when extending the portal.
